parts.title: Art Teacher
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] Art class that meets the curriculum goals for [specific focus, e.g. image-making], including three activities and an introduction.
Give me five strategies to motivate a [grade X] student who feels insecure about drawing during Art lessons, with one encouraging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class a foundational art technique (e.g. [specific technique, e.g. watercolour]), with three steps and a simple project.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. sustainability] into an Art lesson for a [grade X] class, with one art project for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a [grade X] student's self-confidence during an art project.
Give me an example of a routine for starting an Art lesson with a [grade X] class, including three steps and a brief warm-up sketch.
Suggest three ways to use everyday materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. newspapers]) in an Art lesson for a [grade X] class, with one project for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for an Art lesson with a [grade X] class, covering three elements (e.g. reflection, display).
Give me five ideas for making an Art lesson for a [grade X] class more playful, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. imagination], and one activity for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a [grade X] class after an Art lesson on [specific theme, e.g. colour], to capture their thoughts.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with the Music teacher at a K–12 school to connect Art to [specific theme, e.g. rhythm], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's ability in [specific art technique, e.g. perspective], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, artists) for an Art lesson for a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. visual analysis].
Suggest three ways to adapt an Art lesson for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. motor difficulties], with one solution for each.
Create a weekly Art planning template for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. drawing, painting, reflection).
Give me five ideas for using minimal equipment in an Art lesson for a [grade X] class to create [specific project, e.g. a collage], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific art style, e.g. Impressionism], with one hands-on activity for each.
Write a short guide for managing a disruptive Art lesson with a [grade X] class, including three steps and one calming strategy.
Give me an example of an Art lesson for a [grade X] class that combines visual art with [specific theme, e.g. nature], structured in three parts.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the Crafts teacher at a [school for grades X–Y] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. three-dimensional making], with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class in which students design their own poster using [specific technique, e.g. a digital tool], with three steps and one learning goal.
Give me five ways to make a painting task more engaging for a [grade X] class, with one creative twist for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to sketch portraits, with three steps and a simple exercise.
Suggest three ways to connect an Art lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. identity], with one art project for each.
Create a list of five short sketching exercises for a [grade X] class that strengthen [specific goal, e.g. observation], with a description for each.
Give me an example of a routine for closing an Art lesson with a [grade X] class using a mini-exhibition, including three steps.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. Procreate]) in an Art lesson for a [grade X] class, with one task for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection after an Art lesson with a [grade X] class, including three questions about their artistic choices.
Give me five ideas for integrating movement into an Art lesson for a [grade X] class, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. form], and one activity for each.
Create a list of three short ways to support a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific art technique, e.g. mixed media], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with the Social Studies teacher at a school with [grade X–Y] students to connect Art to [specific theme, e.g. history], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to create a comic strip in one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple art projects for a [grade X] class that strengthen [specific goal, e.g. colour understanding], with a brief rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to manage a [grade X] class that loses focus during an Art lesson, with one method for each.
Create a term planning template for Art for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. technique, analysis, creative making) and time frames.
Give me five ideas for creating an art exhibition with a [grade X] class using minimal preparation, with one element for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific art concept, e.g. composition], with one hands-on activity for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. colour blindness] in an Art lesson, including three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson for a [grade X] class that combines Art with [specific theme, e.g. technology], structured in three parts.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the school librarian at a school with [grade X–Y] students to deepen [specific goal, e.g. art history], with one example for each.
